reasoning: The market probability on 2025-02-20 was 0.55. Although 85,000 jobs were reportedly cut by mid-February, the market had this information (published Feb 15) and still only priced the likelihood at 55%. This relatively cautious assessment likely reflects structural challenges: potential court injunctions, pushback from unions and Congress, the possibility that "job losses" in media reports don't perfectly map onto the specific FRED total federal employee series (which includes USPS and fluctuations due to seasonal hiring), and natural attrition dynamics. Since no major definitive new evidence post-Feb 20 completely removes these institutional hurdles, I will align with the market consensus of 0.55.
